parsed_review.summary: CP00-835 is a descriptor-only RP27 Platform Extensibility permission-matrix closeout/handoff bridge pack (risk A, 10 units RP27.P06.M10.S01-S10, distribution 2 security_audit + 8 implementation). Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only. Scope is legitimate and ledger-sourced: weighted-implementation-ledger SHA 13c7ed65... defines RP27.P06.M10 ("Closeout And Next Handoff") as a 20-subphase microphase; this pack covers S01-S10 and the binding/handoff route correctly to CP00-836 / RP27.P06.M10.S11. Implementation is mutually consistent across the contract cp835 projection, registry (PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP835_PACK_BINDING, case-set, descriptor builder, Hermes/Claude packets, closeout handoff), validators (coverage + descriptor), the validation script, and model tests; the 10 rows equal PERMISSION_ROWS.slice(0,10) and match the manifest/plan units and deliverable counts exactly. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: every row is descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, with api_key/webhook/workflow/credential payloads excluded, real_client_data_included=false, permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/writes_product_state=false, and deny-over-allow enforced; the descriptor validator hard-fails on any breach. Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, read_only=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime opening deferred to CP00-836); Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false, i.e. control-plane review-evidence handling only, not product runtime. No premature production_ready/enterprise-trust claim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, and adjudication/inspection/command-evidence are honest pending-review placeholders. The claude-review-result.json 0-byte stdout is the expected pending placeholder (valid_review_receipts=[], exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false), not fraudulent or auth-failed review evidence. Pack-specific command evidence (rp27:platform-extensibility:validate, platform node --test) claims exit 0 and is consistent with the well-formed source I inspected. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic dead-code note only. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block this pack's closeout nor this CP's goal closeout; no unresolved P0/P1. Final production_ready flip and human enterprise-trust approval remain outside this receipt by design.

findings.title: Unreachable closeout-handoff override branch in CP835 case-set builder
findings.details: createPlatformExtensibilityCp835PermissionMatrixCloseoutHandoffBridgeCaseSet() defines closeoutOverride (lines 1814-1817) and applies it only when title === "Closeout handoff" (line 1825). For CP835, required_section_rows["RP27.P06.M10"] = PERMISSION_ROWS.slice(0,10), whose titles are 'Permission matrix row' ... 'Deny-over-allow check'; none equals 'Closeout handoff', so the conditional is never taken and closeoutOverride is effectively unused (carried over from M05-style closeout descriptors). No functional or security impact: the closeout routing is correctly carried by the separate closeout_handoff field via createPlatformExtensibilityCp835CloseoutHandoff() (to_pack_id CP00-836 / next_subphase RP27.P06.M10.S11) and is validated at packages/platform/src/validators.js:1726. The override values themselves are also correct, so even if the branch were reached it would be benign.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup: remove the dead closeoutOverride variable and the title === "Closeout handoff" conditional from this builder to reduce confusion. No change is required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic note. Does not affect the production_ready determination, descriptor correctness, or no-write/no-real-data boundaries. Acknowledged as optional future cleanup; CP00-835 closeout is unaffected.
